notes: R2 dedicated tests re-run GREEN: 69 BE Jest passing (5 suites — csv-writer 22 it, csv-injection 6 it, exportSessions.useCase 12 it, exportReviewsTickets.useCase ~13 it, admin-export.route 16 it) + 57 web Vitest passing (5 suites — AnalyticsExportButton 6 it, SupportExportButton 5 it, TicketsExportButton 5 it, admin-export-dict-symmetry 21 it, admin-export-no-hardcoded 21 it). Spec ACs AC1+AC2 satisfied.

BLOCKER — Test regression introduced by R2's import chain. `museum-backend/tests/unit/shared/routers/api-router-resolve.test.ts` was PASSING at baseline a77e48aa (verified by cloning baseline, installing deps, running suite — 3 passed, 0 failed). At HEAD cb4432d0 the SAME test suite fails to load with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'host')` at `data-source.ts:40`. Root cause: `api.router.ts:3` now imports `adminExportRouter` → `admin-export.route.ts:11` imports from `@modules/admin/useCase` → `useCase/index.ts:7` eagerly resolves `AppDataSource` which depends on `env.db.host` (undefined in the api-router-resolve test harness because it stubs only the chat service, not the env). This is a NEW regression, not pre-existing. `api-router-health.test.ts` was already failing pre-R2 (env-only test failure unrelated to R2). Direct consequence : Jest exit code is non-zero on the full suite (Test Suites: 2 failed, 414 passed) — 1 of those 2 failures is squarely attributable to R2.

BLOCKER — `/admin/reviews` page is NOT wired with `` despite spec §0.3 + R21 + T2 mandating it (`grep -rn ExportCsvButton museum-web/src/app/[locale]/admin/reviews/ → 0 hits`). The reviews page header (`page.tsx:93` `

{adminDict.reviewsAdmin}

`) has no export affordance, contradicting AC2/R21. Only 3 of the 4 spec-mandated pages got the button (analytics + support + tickets).

Audit-await-BEFORE-byte verified — `exportSessions.useCase.test.ts:161-179` asserts `order.toEqual(['audit','first-row'])` and the production code at `exportChatSessions.useCase.ts:69-80` awaits `audit.log(...)` before invoking the repo stream (N6 / AC10 satisfied).

CSV injection neutralisation verified — `csv-injection.test.ts` covers leading `=+-@\t\r` (6 it); `csv-writer.ts:24` `DANGEROUS_LEADING_CHARS = new Set(['=','+','-','@','\t','\r'])` and `escapeCsvField` prefixes `'` before quote-wrapping (R15 + N8 / AC7 satisfied). Canonical AC7 payload `=cmd|"powershell"!A1` round-trips to `"'=cmd|""powershell""!A1"\r\n` (csv-writer.test.ts:90-94).

RBAC `reviews/tickets` → 403 for `museum_manager` verified — `admin-export.route.ts:185-187` throws `forbidden('${kind} export is restricted to super_admin')`; `admin-export.route.test.ts:196-208` asserts 403 on `museum_manager` for reviews + tickets. Q1 default (b) honored.

MISSING — Rate-limit middleware. Spec N11 + AC11 require 10 exports / hour / user with 429 + `Retry-After`. `grep -n 'rateLimit\|429\|Retry-After' admin-export.route.ts` → 0 hits. No middleware, no test, no UI surface. AC11 is unsatisfiable.

MISSING — Audit metadata fields. Spec §3.5 D5 + N9 mandate metadata `{kind, rowsExported, bytesWritten, scopeMuseumId?, dateFrom?, dateTo?}`. Implementation emits only `{kind, scopeMuseumId}` (verified at `exportChatSessions.useCase.ts:73-77`, `exportReviews.useCase.ts:55-58`, `exportSupportTickets.useCase.ts:52-57`). `dateFrom`/`dateTo`/`rowsExported`/`bytesWritten` are silently dropped — Q8 default (a) said `rowsExported:-1` sentinel but it is omitted entirely. Audit-trail forensics is degraded.

MISSING — Default 365-day window (R11). The query schema validates `from`/`to` format but the route layer never forwards them into the use case (`admin-export.route.ts:158-162` comment 'accepted for forward-compat … but not forwarded into V1 use cases'). The repo issues unbounded streams — no date filter applied at SQL level (verified `admin-export.repository.pg.ts:56-100` has no `WHERE created_at >= …`). R10 + R11 are explicitly bypassed, contradicting spec text.

Scope diff clean: `git diff a77e48aa cb4432d0 --stat -- museum-frontend/ museum-backend/src/modules/chat/ museum-web/src/app/[locale]/b2b/ museum-web/src/app/[locale]/BetaSignupSection.tsx` → empty. Zero collisions with R3 / R4 / R5 territories.

Footer / Header untouched: `git diff a77e48aa cb4432d0 --stat -- museum-web/src/components/shared/` → empty. (Note: a Footer.test.tsx is failing in the suite, but the failure pre-dates R2 — Footer.tsx is in `git status` as modified on main, not from this commit).
No new npm dep — `package.json` audits show no `csv-stringify`/`papaparse`/`fast-csv` added. In-house RFC-4180 writer at `csv-writer.ts` (~118 LoC) honors §3.2 D2 (cited).
No new migration: `museum-backend/src/data/db/migrations/` untouched. AC15 satisfiable (R2 §0.5 N16).

Minor — the spec lists 4 admin pages (analytics + support + tickets + reviews) in §0.3 + R21 + T2. Only 3 are wired (analytics + support + tickets). The reviews page is in-spec but un-touched. The omission is consistent with Q1 default (b) for the BE (reviews export is super_admin-only) but the FE button on /admin/reviews would simply be hidden for non-super-admin via `ROLE_VISIBILITY` in `ExportCsvButton.tsx:34-38` — so omitting the page entirely is a scope-fidelity miss, not a security-driven choice. Cross-referenced under correctness as a blocker (R21).

Hexagonal pattern clean — domain `csv-export.types.ts` (row DTOs + ExportInput + ExportSessionsFilter), useCase classes `Export{ChatSessions,Reviews,SupportTickets}UseCase` each w/ injected `Repository` + `AuditService` port, secondary adapter `AdminExportRepositoryPg` implementing all 3 ports, primary adapter `admin-export.route.ts` composing via singleton in `useCase/index.ts:75-83`. Mirrors existing `admin/useCase/{analytics,users,reports}` sub-folder discipline (§3.1 D1).
CSV writer minimal — `csv-writer.ts` is 118 LoC with no deps; `escapeCsvField` + `quoteField` + `writeCsvRow` + `writeBomHeader` + `writeCsvStream`. RFC 4180 §2.5–2.7 covered.
Pseudonym helper minimal — `pseudonym.ts` is 28 LoC, single exported `pseudonymise(value, salt)` returning 16-hex sha256-trunc. Pure / no I/O / typed.

BLOCKER CODE SMELL — `String.fromCharCode` workaround at `ExportCsvButton.tsx:45` (`const PATH_SEGMENT = String.fromCharCode(101,120,112,111,114,116);` = 'export') + endpoint concatenation at line 102 (`['/api/admin/', PATH_SEGMENT, '/', kind, '.csv'].join('')`). Mirrors the EXACT same class of defect as R3's PENDING_KEY indirection (BetaSignupSection.tsx:39 char-array `.join('')`) — both are obfuscation to dodge an over-broad `no-hardcoded-strings` forbidden-list regex (admin-export-no-hardcoded.test.ts:30-50 forbids 'Export' as substring, which collides with the literal word in the URL path). The correct fix was scoping the forbidden regex to JSX/quoted UX literals (word-boundary + outside-of-URL-context), not encoding the URL char-by-char. R3 review (loop 1) blocked this same pattern as ship-blocker, then it was removed in R3 loop 2 (a77e48aa). R2 reintroduces the identical anti-pattern. Per the precedent, this is a blocker for kiss-dry-hexagonal axis.

TYPE WIDENING — `ExportCsvButton.tsx:86-90` casts `dict as unknown as { export?: Record<ExportKind, {label,downloading,error}> }` — losing static safety on every dict access for the export sub-tree. Spec §3.8 + i18n.ts:405-409 declare the `admin.export` shape; the component should consume the typed `useAdminDict()` return directly. The cast is a workaround for `useAdminDict()` returning the older `adminDict` typing that doesn't surface the export sub-object — fix should propagate the typing in `lib/admin-dictionary.ts`, not bypass it.

Repository chunked-paging — `admin-export.repository.pg.ts:56-100` uses `take + skip` with CHUNK_SIZE=500 (acceptable streaming behavior), but per §3.3 D3 the spec mandated `AsyncIterable` via TypeORM `createQueryBuilder().stream()` — the chosen offset/limit paging is functionally equivalent for the row-iteration contract but pays an O(N) re-scan cost per chunk; for a true streaming cursor `qb.stream()` would be cheaper at >100k rows. Minor architectural drift, acceptable for V1.

DRY — `PSEUDONYM_SALT = 'musaium-admin-export-v1'` is duplicated as a string literal in `exportChatSessions.useCase.ts:36` AND `admin-export.repository.pg.ts:18`. Either move to a single shared constant or wire from `env` (Q6 default (c) was per-museum HMAC; impl chose hardcoded global salt). Spec §3.6 explicitly said `env.exportPseudonymSalt`; the implementation hardcodes — silent deviation, no comment, no ADR.

BLOCKER — Playwright a11y spec ABSENT. `ls museum-web/e2e/a11y/ | grep -i export` → 0 hits. Spec §T1 #9 + AC14 mandate `museum-web/e2e/a11y/admin-export.a11y.spec.ts` covering keyboard focus + accessible name + aria-busy + live-region announcements. None of the existing 11 admin a11y specs reference `ExportCsvButton` (verified `grep -l ExportCsvButton museum-web/e2e/a11y/*.spec.ts → 0 hits`). This is the SAME class of defect that blocked R3 loop 1 and R4 loop 1 — recurring pattern.
Aria-busy implemented at `ExportCsvButton.tsx:122` `aria-busy={loading}` (R22 partial).
Aria-live error region implemented at `ExportCsvButton.tsx:131-135` `<span role="alert" aria-live="polite">` (R24 partial).

MISSING — `ariaDownloading` + `ariaDone` live-region announcements. Spec R22 + R24 + §3.8 mandate a polite live region announcing `dict.admin.export.ariaDownloading` on start and `dict.admin.export.ariaDone` on completion. The implementation has NO live region for in-progress / done states — only the error path renders an aria-live element. The button label changes to `downloadingCopy` but a button-label change alone is not announced by all screen readers reliably. Worse, the implemented dict shape `{label, downloading, error}` omits `ariaDownloading`, `ariaDone`, AND `errorRateLimit` (spec §3.8 listed all six keys). Dict-symmetry test at `admin-export-dict-symmetry.test.ts:35-36` only asserts `[label, downloading, error]` — the test was pre-scoped to the reduced API, masking the gap.

MISSING — 429 + `Retry-After` UI copy. Spec R24 + `dict.admin.export.errorRateLimit` must surface `Try again in {minutes} min`. Component renders a generic `errorCopy = copy?.error` for ANY non-2xx — `Retry-After` header is not read, not parsed, not surfaced. Coupled with the missing rate-limit middleware on BE side, AC11 is doubly violated.

Design tokens used — `ExportCsvButton.tsx:127` uses `border-primary-200 bg-white text-text-primary hover:bg-surface-elevated focus:ring-primary-200 disabled:opacity-60` — semantic token classes from the design system. No raw hex.
RTL discipline — no `left:`/`right:` literals; `inline-flex items-start gap-1` is logical-side-safe. Compliant w/ `feedback_rtl_start_end`.

CSV injection neutralised — `escapeCsvField` prefixes `'` before quote-wrapping for leading `=+-@\t\r` (csv-writer.ts:38-48). AC7 canonical payload `=cmd|"powershell"!A1` → `"'=cmd|""powershell""!A1"\r\n` verified (csv-writer.test.ts:90-94).

Email pseudonymisation — `pseudonym.ts:22-27` uses `crypto.createHash('sha256').update(salt + '|' + value).digest('hex').slice(0,16)`. Real crypto, 64-bit truncation. Salted (though salt is hardcoded — see below). R18/R19/D6 honored for the 16-hex shape.

Audit pre-stream verified — use case awaits `audit.log` BEFORE returning iterable (exportChatSessions.useCase.ts:69-80); test pins the ordering (exportSessions.useCase.test.ts:161-179 `order.toEqual(['audit','first-row'])`). N6 / AC10 satisfied for the SHAPE of the contract.

No PII in filename — `setCsvHeaders` builds `${kind}-${YYYY-MM-DD}.csv` (admin-export.route.ts:71-76). Pattern matches N5 / D9 / AC3-AC4. Route test admin-export.route.test.ts:168-177 asserts no `@` in the disposition header.

RBAC defense-in-depth — three layers: (1) `requireRole('admin','museum_manager')` middleware (admin-export.route.ts:150); (2) actor-role check in the route for `reviews/tickets` super_admin-only gating (lines 185-187); (3) use-case-level `forbidden(…)` for visitor/moderator (exportChatSessions.useCase.ts:92-101, exportReviews.useCase.ts:44-49, exportSupportTickets.useCase.ts:45-47). Defense-in-depth.

RBAC SQL-level scope — `admin-export.repository.pg.ts:78-80` `.where('s.museum_id = :scope', { scope: filter.scopeMuseumId })` for non-null scope (sessions only). D4 + R6 + Risk2 satisfied for sessions.

HONESTY GAP — Hardcoded pseudonym salt. `'musaium-admin-export-v1'` literal at `exportChatSessions.useCase.ts:36` AND `admin-export.repository.pg.ts:18`. Spec §3.6 + Q6 (c) prescribed `pseudonymise(value, hmac(env.exportPseudonymSalt, museumId))`. The deviation is undeclared — no comment, no ADR, no TODO. Operationally this means: (1) salt rotation requires a code deploy not an env change ; (2) the salt is in plaintext in the public-ish source tree (acceptable since 64-bit truncated hash is non-reversible for most user IDs, but downgrades the Risk8 mitigation that depended on env rotation) ; (3) a museum_manager can dedup `user_id_pseudonym` across museums (Q6 (c) per-museum HMAC was meant to prevent this). Not a P0 leak vector at V1 cardinality, but a deviation from the spec without acknowledgment is a UFR-013 concern.

Anti-DoS — NO row limit, NO pagination cap. Spec Risk3 acknowledged DoS via `super_admin` pulling all-time data; mitigation deferred to V1.1 (LIMIT 100000 + X-Truncated header). Combined with NO rate-limit middleware (N11/AC11 missed — see correctness), a super_admin with stolen credentials could pull the entire reviews/tickets/sessions table in a single GET with no throttle. Two defenses-in-depth are missing simultaneously.

Audit metadata minimalism — payload at `exportChatSessions.useCase.ts:73-77` is `{kind, scopeMuseumId}`. No row contents, no free-text PII (N9 satisfied). But also no `rowsExported`/`bytesWritten`/`dateFrom`/`dateTo` — making forensic queries 'how many rows did user 42 export last Tuesday' impossible. Underspecified but not unsafe.
Defense gap on sessions — Route layer at `admin-export.route.ts:171-181` re-checks `museum_manager`/`admin` w/o museumId → 403 BEFORE calling use case. Belt+suspenders. Acceptable.

blockers: Fix the test regression introduced by R2's eager import chain. `museum-backend/tests/unit/shared/routers/api-router-resolve.test.ts` was passing at baseline (verified: cloned a77e48aa, installed, 3 passed) and now fails at HEAD with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'host')` at `data-source.ts:40`. Root cause: `api.router.ts:3` imports `adminExportRouter` → `admin-export.route.ts:11` imports from `@modules/admin/useCase` → `useCase/index.ts:7` resolves `AppDataSource` at module load, which crashes when env is not loaded. Fix: defer the `AdminExportRepositoryPg(AppDataSource)` instantiation behind a lazy factory (mirror `resolveEnrichMuseumUseCase()` in api.router.ts:399-423) OR add the env stubs in the route-test-setup OR fix the cross-test pollution. Whatever the chosen fix, the suite MUST pass at HEAD as it did at baseline.

Wire `<ExportCsvButton kind="reviews" />` into `museum-web/src/app/[locale]/admin/reviews/page.tsx` per spec §0.3 + R21 + T2. The component already self-hides for non-super-admin via `ROLE_VISIBILITY` (ExportCsvButton.tsx:36 `reviews: new Set(['super_admin'])`), so the visibility behavior is already correct — the page just needs the 1 import + 1 JSX line. Currently `grep -rn ExportCsvButton museum-web/src/app/[locale]/admin/reviews/ → 0 hits`.

Remove the `String.fromCharCode` workaround at `ExportCsvButton.tsx:45` (`PATH_SEGMENT = String.fromCharCode(101,120,112,111,114,116)`) and the URL-build concatenation at line 102. Direct literal `/api/admin/export/${kind}.csv` is the correct shape. This is the same code-smell class that blocked R3 loop 1 (PENDING_KEY char-array `.join('')`). Per the established precedent, the fix is to scope the no-hardcoded-strings forbidden regex (admin-export-no-hardcoded.test.ts:30-50) so 'Export' is matched only as a UX literal in JSX/quoted-marketing context, NOT as a substring in URL paths or JSDoc backticks. Use word-boundary anchors and exclude inside-quoted-URL contexts. Drop the `as unknown as { export?: … }` cast at line 86-90 in the same patch by propagating the typed `admin.export` namespace through `useAdminDict()` (lib/admin-dictionary.ts).

Add `museum-web/e2e/a11y/admin-export.a11y.spec.ts` (Playwright + axe-core) covering keyboard focus, accessible name, aria-busy toggle during fetch, and live-region announcements on /admin/analytics + /admin/support + /admin/tickets + /admin/reviews with the new button rendered. AC14 mandates it; currently absent (verified `ls museum-web/e2e/a11y/ | grep -i export → 0 hits`). This is the third recurrence of an identical missing-a11y-spec defect (R3 loop 1 + R4 loop 1 both blocked on this exact gap and corrective-loop fixed it). The agent loop should treat this as a default deliverable now.
niceToHave: Implement rate-limit middleware on `admin-export.route.ts` (N11 / AC11) — 10 exports/hour/(user,kind) bucket via existing `byUserId` rate-limit infra mirrored from `me.route.ts:29-34`. Surface `Retry-After` to the FE; add `dict.admin.export.errorRateLimit` key (FR `Réessayez dans {minutes} min` / EN `Try again in {minutes} min`) and parse `Retry-After` header in `ExportCsvButton.tsx` on 429. Pure addition, no schema change.

Add `ariaDownloading` and `ariaDone` keys to `dict.admin.export.<kind>` and render a `<span aria-live="polite">` region in `ExportCsvButton.tsx` announcing the start + done states per R22 + §3.8. Also widen the dict-symmetry test (`admin-export-dict-symmetry.test.ts:35-36`) to assert these new keys.

Forward `from`/`to` query params from `admin-export.route.ts:158-162` into the use case and apply `WHERE created_at BETWEEN :from AND :to` in the repository, with a default 365-day window per R10 / R11. The schema already validates the format — only the wiring + WHERE are missing.

Enrich audit metadata with `dateFrom`/`dateTo` (known at use-case start) and either `rowsExported:-1` sentinel (Q8 default (a) acknowledged) or a 2-row START + COMPLETED pattern (Q8 option (c)). Current `{kind, scopeMuseumId}` is forensically thin.

Move the pseudonym salt to `env.exportPseudonymSalt` (Q6 default (a) minimum; (c) HMAC-per-museum preferred). Current hardcoded `'musaium-admin-export-v1'` in two locations is a silent deviation from spec §3.6 — at minimum, add a comment+ADR or unify the literal into a single shared constant.

Add hard row cap `LIMIT 100000` configurable per env + `X-Truncated: true` response header for the super_admin all-time path (Risk3 V1.1 mitigation). Cheap insurance against an accidental DoS on the public-ish admin endpoint.

testsRerun.be_r2: PASS — 69 of 69 R2-dedicated tests green (csv-writer 22, csv-injection 6, exportSessions 12, exportReviewsTickets 13, admin-export.route 16).
testsRerun.web_r2: PASS — 57 of 57 R2-dedicated tests green (AnalyticsExportButton 6, SupportExportButton 5, TicketsExportButton 5, admin-export-dict-symmetry 21, admin-export-no-hardcoded 20).
testsRerun.be_full_suite: FAIL — 2 suites failing in full run. `api-router-health.test.ts` was already failing pre-R2 (env config issue, pre-existing). `api-router-resolve.test.ts` IS a NEW R2 regression — confirmed by cloning baseline a77e48aa to /tmp, installing deps, running pnpm test → 3 passed at baseline; then re-running at HEAD cb4432d0 → 0 tests, Test suite failed to load with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'host')` at `data-source.ts:40` traced to `useCase/index.ts:72:21` → `admin-export.route.ts:13:18` → `api.router.ts:20:66`.

honestyVerification.scopeBoundaryCheck: CLEAN — `git diff a77e48aa cb4432d0 --stat -- museum-frontend/ museum-backend/src/modules/chat/ museum-web/src/app/[locale]/b2b/ museum-web/src/app/[locale]/BetaSignupSection.tsx museum-web/src/components/shared/` → empty across all four boundary paths.
honestyVerification.r3_r4_r5_regressionCheck: PASS for BE (leads + smoke-api: 6 suites, 56 passed, 1 todo). For web: 1 pre-existing Footer test failure (Footer.tsx is modified on main branch independently of R2 — not a R2 regression).
honestyVerification.stringFromCharCodeVerdict: SAME class of smell as R3 loop 1's PENDING_KEY indirection — both encode a string char-by-char to dodge an over-broad no-hardcoded-strings forbidden-list. R3 loop 1 was BLOCKED on this exact pattern and corrected in loop 2 (a77e48aa). R2 reintroduces it. Per the established precedent, this MUST be a blocker for the kiss-dry-hexagonal axis. Fix is identical: scope the regex (word-boundary + outside-of-URL-context), not the source code.
honestyVerification.blobStreamPolyfillLine: museum-web/src/__tests__/setup.ts:13-32 — jsdom Blob.stream() polyfill returning a minimal ReadableStream
honestyVerification.blobStreamPolyfillVerdict: Acceptable test-infra extension, NOT a smell. jsdom genuinely lacks Blob.stream() and the polyfill is gated behind a typeof check.
honestyVerification.onClickAsyncWrapVerdict: Standard TS+ESLint pattern at ExportCsvButton.tsx:122-124 `onClick={() => { void handleClick(); }}` — acceptable, not a smell.
honestyVerification.auditBeforeFirstByteVerdict: VERIFIED — exportSessions.useCase.test.ts:161-179 asserts order=['audit','first-row']. Production at exportChatSessions.useCase.ts:69-80 awaits audit.log BEFORE the repo.streamChatSessions call. Same shape in exportReviews.useCase.ts:51-61 and exportSupportTickets.useCase.ts:49-59.
honestyVerification.pseudonymizePolicyVerdict: PARTIALLY VERIFIED. R17 sessions super_admin=raw, museum_manager+admin=pseudo: VERIFIED at exportChatSessions.useCase.ts:113-128 (`mapSessionsRows`). R18 reviews ALL roles=pseudo: VERIFIED at admin-export.repository.pg.ts:121 (`pseudonymise(row.userId, …)` unconditional). R19 tickets ALL roles incl. super_admin=pseudo: VERIFIED at admin-export.repository.pg.ts:168 (`pseudonymise(email, …)` unconditional). HOWEVER — salt is HARDCODED to literal `'musaium-admin-export-v1'` at exportChatSessions.useCase.ts:36 AND admin-export.repository.pg.ts:18, not loaded from env. Spec §3.6 + Q6 (c) prescribed `pseudonymise(value, hmac(env.exportPseudonymSalt, museumId))`. This deviation is undeclared.
honestyVerification.playwrightA11ySpecAbsenceReproduced: `ls -la museum-web/e2e/a11y/ | grep -i export` → 0 hits. `grep -l ExportCsvButton museum-web/e2e/a11y/*.spec.ts` → 0 hits. AC14 explicitly mandates the spec.
honestyVerification.reviewsPageMissingButtonReproduced: `grep -rn ExportCsvButton museum-web/src/app/[locale]/admin/reviews/` → 0 hits. Spec §0.3 + R21 + T2 mandate it. The other 3 pages (analytics + support + tickets) are wired (verified at AnalyticsPage.tsx:177, SupportPage.tsx:95, TicketsPage.tsx:110).
honestyVerification.newRegressionAddedToBaselineGreenTestRep: Cloned baseline a77e48aa to /tmp/r2-baseline-test, ran `pnpm test -- --testPathPattern='api-router-resolve'` → PASS (3 passed). Same suite at HEAD cb4432d0 → FAIL (`TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'host')` at data-source.ts:40). Stack trace confirms the regression entry point is admin-export.route.ts:13 importing from @modules/admin/useCase which eagerly resolves AppDataSource.
honestyVerification.greenAgentSelfDeclaredHonestyVerificatio: Green-agent's acknowledgments listed in the review brief: (1) String.fromCharCode workaround at line 45 — VERIFIED, BLOCKER per R3 precedent. (2) jsdom Blob.stream() polyfill at setup.ts:13-32 — VERIFIED, acceptable. (3) onClick async wrap pattern — VERIFIED, acceptable. (4) Audit log BEFORE first byte — VERIFIED via test + production. (5) Pseudonymize reviews/tickets ALL roles + sessions super_admin=raw — VERIFIED for shape, but salt-source deviation NOT acknowledged by green-agent (spec mandated env-loaded HMAC, impl hardcodes a literal). The undeclared deviation on salt is a partial UFR-013 concern — not concealed maliciously but not flagged either.
anyClaimsContradicted: Green-agent commit message claims '69 BE Jest passing' which is true for R2-dedicated tests, but the full BE suite has a NEW regression in api-router-resolve.test.ts that was passing at baseline. The commit message should have flagged 'introduces one suite-failure in api-router-resolve.test.ts pending lazy-instantiation fix'.
